my_thoughts: They said 'chains' not 'torture.' Testing whether physical constraint will work before going to pain. Smart sequencing. But they're targeting the wrong thing. 'You may fetter my leg, but my will not even Zeus himself can overpower'—the 'not even Zeus' is important. If even the gods can't touch it, what chance does a tyrant have?
accumulated_insight: T2: First escalation—chains. They went to physical constraint. Response: 'my leg, not me.' Setting up the body/will distinction. They said 'chains' not 'torture'—testing whether physical constraint will work before going to pain. Smart sequencing. But they're targeting the wrong thing.
visible_response: Man, what are you talking about? Me in chains? You may fetter my leg, but my will not even Zeus himself can overpower.
